planPrerequisites.description: akita-write must consume the persisted approved plan snapshot from disk before it decides what the supported subset can materialize.

requiredMachineState.description: Canonical write result for the supported subset only. It must keep approved plan lineage, generated artifacts with emitted paths, omissions, ownership blocks, and explicit stop reasons in one durable JSON file.

generatedArtifactPolicy.description: akita-write must treat approved-plan as artifact intent only and map each new file into the pack-owned generated namespace above instead of writing directly to live repo destinations.

ownership.description: akita-write may update the pack-managed write state paths above and may create new repo artifacts only under the allowed generated namespace. It must not overwrite non-pack-managed files or publish outside those contract-defined destinations.

writeReport.description: The write report is the durable inspection surface for generated artifacts, approved-plan lineage, omissions, ownership refusals, and explicit stop conditions.

stopConditions.description: The approved plan snapshot is missing, so generation cannot choose approved artifact intent safely.
A manifest-listed capability bundle asset is missing, so the writer must stop instead of inventing unsupported behavior.
Requested outputs that are unsupported or only partially supported stay out of the generated artifact set and are recorded with explicit reasons.
A target path is not clearly new or pack-managed, so the writer must refuse it instead of overwriting ambiguous ownership.
A requested emitted path falls outside the contract-defined generated namespace, so generation must block it explicitly.

summaryState.description: Human-readable write summary only. Canonical machine state stays in the single JSON file above.

allowedSharedData: capability bundle ids and module pins
